document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer clinical evaluation report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under EU MDR clinical evaluation.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation, equivalence, safety, performance, benefit-risk, and undesirable side-effect assessment for initial CE marking technical documentation.
keyPoints: The report was prepared according to MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex XIV.
The device is described as a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children.
The device is stated to be undergoing initial CE marking and not yet placed on the market.
The report classifies the device as class IIb under Rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The report claims equivalence to DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 and compares clinical, technical, and biological characteristics.
The report concludes that residual risks are acceptable and that the device is substantially equivalent to marketed predicate devices.

concepts.name: Clinical Evaluation
concepts.evidence: This report is made in accordance with the MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.

concepts.name: EU MDR General Safety and Performance Requirements
concepts.evidence: In this clinical evaluation report, we conclude that the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, is compliance to General Safety and Performance Requirements 1, General Safety and Performance Requirement 2, and General Safety and Performance Requirement 8.

concepts.name: MDR Annex VIII Rule 10
concepts.evidence: Devices specifically intended for recording of diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation are classified as class IIb.

concepts.name: Class IIb medical device
concepts.evidence: Therefore, this device is classified as class IIb in accordance with the rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7 / 1 Rev.4.

concepts.name: Post-market Surveillance
concepts.evidence: To evaluate residual risk on our device, long term study through Post-market Surveillance is necessary.

concepts.name: Post Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: The PMCF will be conducted as a part of the PMS activities.

concepts.name: Detective quantum efficiency
concepts.evidence: Clinical outcome parameter Detective quantum efficiency (DQE)

concepts.name: Modulation transfer function
concepts.evidence: Clinical outcome parameter Modulation transfer function (MTF)

evidence.claim: The document is Clinical Evaluation Report A-TCF-5-118, revision 3, dated April 12, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-118 Rev. No. 3 Clinical Evaluation Report Rev. Date April 12, 2024

evidence.claim: The evaluated product is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with model names A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on April 12, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Han, Jimin Quality team April 12, 2024 Reviewed by Lee, Dongjin R&D manager April 12, 2024 Reviewed by Kim, Sunghyun Clinical evaluator (M.D., Ph.D.) April 12, 2024 Approved by Hur, Namkyu Quality manager April 12, 2024

evidence.claim: Revision 3 updated the number and issue date of the RED test certificate for the RF module.
evidence.evidenceText: 3 April 12, 2024 Update the number and issue date of RED test certificate for RF module

evidence.claim: The primary purpose of the report is to evaluate safety and performance conformity, acceptable benefit-risk ratio, and acceptability of undesirable side-effects under EU MDR GSPR 1 and 8.
evidence.evidenceText: The primary purpose of this report is to evaluate conformity assessment with requirement on safety and performance and acceptable benefit-risk ratio (Regulation (EU) 2017/745 GSPR 1), and acceptability of undesirable side-effects (Regulation (EU) 2017/745 GSPR 8).

evidence.claim: The report states it was made in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex XIV.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is made in accordance with the MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Name: H&abyz Co., Ltd. Address: 2-Dong, 41-16, Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-eup, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, 17118, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The device is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector designed for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The device consists of TFT, CsI scintillator, and electronics that convert read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.

evidence.claim: The device supports auto exposure detection technology without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The device is undergoing initial CE marking.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is undergoing initial CE-marking

evidence.claim: The product was not yet placed on the market at the time of the report.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is not placed on the market yet.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, replacing film-based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient populations Adult and children except infants and pregnant women
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Population

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, fluoroscopy, angiography, infants, or pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The device is reusable and has no known side effects according to the report.
evidence.evidenceText: Single use / reusable Reusable Side-effects The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW have no known side effects.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Safety

evidence.claim: The device is designed for approximately five years of service life.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Service Life

evidence.claim: The report classifies the device as class IIb under Rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, this device is classified as class IIb in accordance with the rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The report identifies DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 as CE-marked equivalent devices.
evidence.evidenceText: DRX-1 of Carestream Health (CE marked by NB 0086) CXDI-70C of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197) CXDI-31 of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197)

evidence.claim: The report states that clinical, technical, and biological characteristics were compared between the device and DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical, technical and biological characteristics comparison between ‘Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector’ and CE marking devices ‘DRX-1’ and ‘CXDI-70C’ and ‘CXDI-31’ was analyzed.

evidence.claim: The report concludes that DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 are equivalent to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector despite non-identical features.
evidence.evidenceText: we concluded that the CE indicators ‘DRX-1’ and ‘CXDI-70C’ and ‘CXDI-31’ are equivalent to ‘Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector’, although our products are not exactly the same in appearance (see 1.1), Scintillator (1.6), etc.

evidence.claim: Clinical images were acquired for 30 sites and evaluated by a qualified clinical evaluator.
evidence.evidenceText: In addition, clinical images were acquired for 30 sites and evaluated by a qualified Clinical Evaluator.

evidence.claim: The report lists ADD as the benchmark and previous-generation device.
evidence.evidenceText: The benchmark device, which is previous generations of the device of A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW, is ADD.

evidence.claim: The device under evaluation is an indirect energy conversion detector.
evidence.evidenceText: These proposed devices are the detectors with indirect energy conversion.

evidence.claim: The report identifies screen-film radiography and imaging plate computed radiography as alternative treatments.
evidence.evidenceText: Our device is an Flat Panel Detector for Digital Radiography and there are alternative treatments like Screen-film(X-ray film) for Conventional Radiography (Screen-film Radiography, Film-screen Radiography) and Imaging Plate for Computed Radiography.

evidence.claim: Biocompatibility tests were performed for cytotoxicity, irritation, and sensitization on the deco sheet.
evidence.evidenceText: Name of the test Cytotoxicity study Testing standard ISO 10993-5 ... Name of the test Irritation study Testing standard ISO 10993-10 ... Name of the test Sensitization study Testing standard ISO 10993-10

evidence.claim: The report states 29 risks were identified and analyzed in the risk management report prepared in December 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: A-TCF-5-104 Risk management report was prepared in December 2022, and there were 29 risks identified and analyzed.

evidence.claim: The report states performance testing was conducted to demonstrate intended performance.
evidence.evidenceText: The ability of a medical device to achieve its intended purpose as claimed by the manufacturer needs to be demonstrated so Performance Test was conducted by KOLAS, an accredited testing laboratory.

evidence.claim: The RED test was performed on Wi-Fi Transceiver model WBW880A with report number 0470-RED-185003 issued March 19, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: RED Test Information of Tested sample for Wi-Fi Transceiver Model WBW880A ... Issued date of the test report 2024-03-19 Test report No. 0470-RED-185003

evidence.claim: The report states there are no side effects and risks have been controlled to be acceptable through risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: There are no side effects, and the risks that may occur have been controlled to be acceptable through risk A-TCF-5-104 management report.

evidence.claim: The PMCF will collect customer survey data and clinical evaluation data every year.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the PMCF plan (A-PMCFP-5-001 PMCF plan), customer survey data and clinical evaluation data will be collected, analyzed and reported every year.

evidence.claim: The report identifies reduction in patient dose and high quality image as Clinical benefit 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 1 – Reduction in patient dose and high quality image

evidence.claim: The report identifies fast image acquisition time as Clinical benefit 2.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 2 - Fast image acquisition time

evidence.claim: The report concludes the expected clinical benefit can be achieved and benefits outweigh risks.
evidence.evidenceText: We have confirmed that the expected clinical benefit of Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) can be achieved through clinical data. The benefits of our device outweigh the risks.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the device is substantially equivalent to marketed predicate devices and raises no new safety or effectiveness issues.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on the above, we conclude that the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is substantially equivalent to the marketed predicate device, and do not raise any new issues of safety or effectiveness.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report update frequency is every year.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every year.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but the source text primarily supports EU MDR, CE marking, and notified body context; MFDS appears only as a recall-search source.

Potential conflicting classification evidence: the classification table marks 'Device intended for recording of X-ray diagnostic images?' as 'N', but the classification rationale applies MDR Annex VIII Rule 10 for devices intended for recording diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation.

Potential conflicting A/D conversion evidence: technical specification states A/D Conversion is 16 bits, while the equivalence table row for the device under clinical evaluation lists A/D Conversion as 14 bits.
The source is confidential manufacturer technical documentation, not an issued certificate or regulator-authored decision.
